column1: # Recovery control variant surface. Columns are:
# `core` is state retained across recovery steps. `transition` is an intent, plan, result, or
# projection consumed at a boundary. `support` is a recovery error/diagnostic classification and
# is tracked separately so it cannot inflate the core-lifecycle total.
# Counts are exact: reductions lower this file, while increases require an intentional review.
# Every enum anywhere under src/ whose name contains `Recover` or starts with `Resume`, `Reconnect`,
# or `Restore` must have a row. In a file already represented here, control/result suffixes are
# also discovered so helper names cannot evade the budget; add new IPC types and their projections
# This is a typed-enum ratchet, not a generic bool/Option detector. `check-architecture.sh` guards
# known typed boundaries, but there is currently no general implicit-control-debt scanner.
# Typed transition, plan, mode, and result projections.
# Recovery classifications which are not retained lifecycle state.
